# Planning Brief - 2026-06-29

Source: `.github/research/2026-06-28-research.md` (most recent research)

## Headline

Today's brief is a **queue-recovery brief**, not a new-issue brief.

`gh issue list --label auto-implement --state open` returned **0** at
the start of this run, which is what the research file
(`2026-06-28-research.md`) also reported. Direct inspection of
`gh issue view` on the open backlog showed four actionable, fully
specified issues -- #862, #864, #867, #869 -- carrying only
`enhancement`. The `auto-implement` label silently dropped during
yesterday's `gh issue create` (the engineering pipeline keys off
`labels=auto-implement`, so the queue was invisible).

All four issues have been re-labelled. Net result: 0 new issues
created, +4 actionable items on the engineering queue, +1 ops
finding to forward to `role-management` so this label-loss does
not recur.

## Open queue after this run

`gh issue list --label auto-implement --state open` now returns:

| ---- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | -------- | ----- | --------------------------------------------------- |

| #864 | [command] /queue list and /queue clear for chained follow-up prompts             | command  | small | research 2026-06-27 (cline #11875)                  |
| #867 | [provider] formatProviderError preserves error.cause on rethrow                  | provider | small | research 2026-06-28 (cline #11928)                  |
| #869 | [config] Compaction: prefer recorded tokens + reserve output budget + 80% target | config   | small | research 2026-06-28 (cline #11894)                  |

Total: 4 actionable items. Below the 5-issue cap; today's research
brief produced zero new actionable items (the three "NEW" items it
listed are the same three that became #867/#868/#869 yesterday;
#868 already shipped in PR #871).

## Execution order for the engineering pipeline

The research brief sets the order: drain prior items first, then
ship today's items in low-risk -> hot-path order. Apply to the
current queue:

1. **#867** (provider error cause, ~2h, isolated new file).
2. **#864** (slash command, ~3h, REPL-local).
3. **#862** (MCP name sanitisation, ~3h, two-file touch with a
   small reverse-map but no router/orchestrator coupling).
4. **#869** (compaction token budgeting, ~3h, hot path -- last so
   regressions caught by earlier shipped work don't pile up).

`auto-implement.yml` picks by oldest-first today, which gives the
same order naturally (#862 < #864 < #867 < #869).

## Items deliberately NOT made into issues

- **claude-code (8 cycles dry) and aider (31 days silence)**:
  cadence problem, not a code item. Belongs to `role-management`
  patching `agent1-research-system.md` to retag both repos
  `<!-- monthly (1st Mon) -->`. No `auto-implement` issue.
- **opencode #34171 (V2Event SSE) and #34167 (empty allowlist)**:
  sst/effect internals; the latter is already match-any in
  `src/core/router.ts` per the research note.
- **Re-create #867 / #868 / #869 / #862 / #864 as "new" items**:
  these are direct duplicates by `What` of issues that already
  exist in the open backlog. The role rules forbid this:
  "do not bypass duplicate detection. Open issues with the same
  `What` must be detected and skipped." Creating duplicates would
  also confuse Daily PR Merge's `Closes #N` tracking.

## Ops finding: label-loss on `gh issue create --label "a,b"`

When yesterday's planning agent ran
`gh issue create --label "auto-implement,enhancement" --body-file ...`
the resulting issues showed only `enhancement`. Same behaviour
visible on #862, #864, #867, #869. The reproduction is consistent
with `gh` parsing the comma list as a single label name when one
of the labels does not already exist *in the repo at the time of
the API call*; but `auto-implement` does exist (`gh label list`
confirms color `#0E8A16`). The likely cause is a `gh` version that
drops unknown-to-the-cache labels silently when the issue template
adds its own default label set.

`agent2-planning-system.md` already mandates a post-create verify
step (`gh issue view <num> --json labels | grep auto-implement`)
and a recovery step (`gh issue edit <num> --add-label auto-implement`).
Yesterday's agent skipped that step. Recommendation for
`role-management`: harden the rule by replacing the two-call
pattern with a single `gh issue create --label auto-implement
--label enhancement` form (separate `--label` flags, not
comma-joined), and keep the verify+repair fallback in place. This
is a one-line change to `agent2-planning-system.md` and to
`auto-implement.yml`'s issue-creation snippets; tracked in the
ops note attached to today's commit, NOT a code issue (since the
role rules forbid this vertical from touching workflows).

## Codebase validation notes

Validating the proposed file paths in #862, #864, #867, #869 still
match HEAD:

- `src/core/orchestrator.ts` -- still has the `throw err;` rethrow
  site (issue #867).
- `src/core/streamingOrchestrator.ts` -- still has the second
  rethrow site (issue #867).
- `src/providers/format.ts` -- absent (issue #867 will create it).
  `tests/providers/` exists with sibling tests; new test file
  `tests/providers/format.test.ts` lands cleanly.
- `src/cli/interactive.ts` -- still contains the `today` / `month`
  / `all` branches in the `/cost` slash handler (issue #868 ship
  confirmed this layout; issue #864 adds a `/queue` branch in the
  same `switch`).
- `src/cli/tui/components/StatusBar.tsx` -- present; #868 shipped
  the `topModelLabel` prop already, so #864 does not collide.
- `src/core/compaction.ts` -- present, untouched since the brief
  was written (issue #869).
- `src/mcp/client.ts` -- present (issue #862).
- `tests/setup.ts` is still intentionally empty; new tests in
  these issues use local `vi.mock` + `fs.mkdtemp` patterns, matching
  the project convention in AGENTS.md.

## Risk and ordering

- **#867 (provider error cause)**: lowest risk. New file +
  `.message` rewrite at two rethrow sites. Cause-recursion is
  depth-bounded at 2.
- **#864 (/queue command)**: REPL-local; no orchestrator coupling.
  Only risk is leaking a stale preview if `enqueueSubmitPrompt`
  changes the queue shape. Mitigation: reuse the existing 80-char
  preview helper.
- **#862 (MCP tool name sanitisation)**: touches the MCP call-tool
  path. Mitigation: keep raw->sanitised lookup map per connection
  and only rewrite at the boundary; existing `permission` keys
  already use the `mcp__server__tool` shape.
- **#869 (compaction)**: hot path; changes a shipped function.
  Mitigation already in the issue body: keep legacy positional
  `shouldCompact(messages, max, threshold)` working, only apply
  80%-target logic when the new options bag is passed. Existing
  `SessionManager.checkAndCompact` call site stays on legacy path
  in this issue; a follow-up wires the bag.

## Why no new issues this run

Today's research brief contains:

- Three items (cline #11928, kilocode #11608, cline #11894) which
  are the same three that became #867 / #868 / #869 yesterday.
  #868 shipped (PR #871, merged 2026-06-28). #867 and #869 are now
  in the queue. Filing them again would create duplicates.
- Two opencode items the brief itself defers.
- A cadence demotion proposal (claude-code + aider monthly) that
  is not an `auto-implement` code item.

Inflating today's batch with speculative items would dilute backlog
quality and contradict the role's prioritisation rule
("3-5 issues" is a CAP, not a floor). The queue currently holds
~11h of small work; engineering can keep shipping for the next
two cycles without new input.

## Deferred / not in this batch

- Cadence demotion of claude-code + aider to monthly -- not a code
  change. Tracked for `role-management`.
- Wiring `reserveOutputTokens` into `SessionManager.checkAndCompact`
  -- intentional follow-up so #869 stays small and reviewable.
- Hardening `agent2-planning-system.md` and `auto-implement.yml` to
  use `--label auto-implement --label enhancement` (separate flags)
  -- workflow change, not in this vertical's surface.
